overview: Sultai Reanimator is a Tier 2 Standard combo deck that leverages Universes Beyond cards to cheat large threats into play. It primarily uses Superior Spider-Man and Bringer of the Last Gift to assemble a massive board state, often finishing games with Ardyn, the Usurper's combat triggers. While currently sitting behind top-tier decks like Izzet Vivi Cauldron and Red Deck Wins, it offers a high upside for players who enjoy graveyard-based strategies.
win_conditions: Casting Superior Spider-Man to copy Bringer of the Last Gift and return the entire graveyard to the battlefield
Using Ardyn, the Usurper to turn reanimated utility creatures into 5/5s and boost all demons for a lethal combat swing
Assembling a wide board of demons and utility creatures to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize

keep_criteria: Hands containing Superior Spider-Man alongside cheap graveyard fillers or cantrips
Hands with multiple low-cost utility creatures and Proft's Eidetic Memory
Keep any hand with a land, a turn-1 discard spell (e.g., Deceit), and a graveyard enabler or threat.
Prioritize keeping 3-4 lands if you have a turn-1 discard and a tutor/graveyard enabler, as you need to cast disruption immediately to protect your game plan.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking Superior Spider-Man or a reliable way to interact with the graveyard
Hands composed only of high-cost threats without early game acceleration
Mulligan hands with two or more lands, no early disruption, or only reanimation spells without a threat or graveyard setup.

examples.reason: Contains the primary win condition with cheap graveyard fillers and cantrips to dig for the rest of the combo.
Lacks Superior Spider-Man and early game pieces, making it too slow to execute the combo before opponent interaction.

early_game: Play lands and cheap utility creatures like Kavaero, Mind-Bitten or Steamcore Scholar
Use Proft's Eidetic Memory to dig for Superior Spider-Man and Bringer of the Last Gift
Avoid unnecessary combat to preserve creatures for the graveyard
Turn 1: Play a land, cast Deceit to disrupt the opponent's hand or set up your graveyard.
Turn 2: Play a second land, dig for your reanimation engine or primary threat.
If you have a threat in hand and a reanimation spell in the graveyard, sequence mana to cast the reanimation spell on turn 2 or 3.
Land sequencing: Start with untapped dual or shock lands when possible to ensure turn-1 disruption, but prioritize basic lands if they guarantee you can cast your early spells.
mid_game: Cast Superior Spider-Man to copy Bringer of the Last Gift
Trigger Bringer to return the entire graveyard, including Ardyn, the Usurper
Prepare to swing for lethal on the following turn
Treat graveyard resources as a shared pool.
Don't overcommit to reanimating low-impact threats if you can save the mana for a game-winning threat.
Keep 2-3 cards in hand to answer unexpected threats or protect your graveyard.
Mana should be hoarded for reanimation windows, but spent on disruption if the opponent is ahead or developing a threatening board.
Commit threats when you have graveyard redundancy or can protect them with counterspells/removal.
Hold back if the opponent has board wipes, graveyard hate, or significant removal.
late_game: Execute the OTK combat phase with Ardyn's 5/5 triggers and boosted demons
Use remaining resources to stabilize if the combo is interrupted
Look for alternate win conditions like Overlord of the Balemurk if the primary plan fails
Superior Spider-Man and Overlord of the Balemurk are your primary win conditions.
Reanimate them when you have a clear path to damage or when the opponent cannot interact.
Use discard spells to clear the way or force the opponent to discard key answers, then close the game with unblockable or resilient threats.
Go all-in when you have a reanimated threat on the board, the opponent has no graveyard hate or board wipes in play, and you have enough mana to protect your threat or close the game.
Also commit if you can win before the opponent's next turn or if they are tapped out from trying to stabilize against your early pressure.

key_cards.name: Superior Spider-Man
key_cards.notes: Copies Bringer of the Last Gift to return the entire graveyard to the battlefield.

key_cards.name: Bringer of the Last Gift
key_cards.notes: Returns all creatures from the graveyard when copied by Superior Spider-Man.

key_cards.name: Ardyn, the Usurper
key_cards.notes: Reanimates utility creatures as 5/5s and boosts all demons during combat.

key_cards.name: Overlord of the Balemurk
key_cards.notes: Provides an alternate path to victory if the primary combo is disrupted.

key_cards.name: Valgavoth, Terror Eater
key_cards.notes: A large demon that benefits from Ardyn's combat boost and can swing for lethal.

key_cards.name: Proft's Eidetic Memory
key_cards.notes: Draws cards to locate Superior Spider-Man and Bringer of the Last Gift.

sequencing_tips: Cast Superior Spider-Man targeting a copy of Bringer of the Last Gift to trigger the mass return effect
Ensure Ardyn, the Usurper is in the graveyard before casting Bringer so it returns to the battlefield
Wait until the beginning of combat to trigger Ardyn's ability, reanimating a utility creature as a 5/5
Swing with Bringer of the Last Gift and all other demons simultaneously to maximize Ardyn's combat boost
Common mistake: overcommitting to reanimating low-impact threats early, leaving you vulnerable to board wipes or graveyard hate.
Always keep a backup plan in hand.
Unique interaction: Deceit can be used to both disrupt the opponent and set up your own graveyard, so sequence it carefully to maximize value.
Superior Spider-Man and Overlord of the Balemurk likely have synergies with your graveyard engine
test their specific interactions to maximize value.
In an unknown format, always be prepared to adapt your sideboard based on the actual metagame shifts, and track opponent play patterns to predict when they will interact with your graveyard.

matchups.advice: Prioritize speed and try to close out games before they establish a wide board of tokens and removal.
watch_for: Removal spells targeting your key threats

matchups.advice: Play around early aggression and use your utility creatures to block or trade efficiently while setting up the combo.
Use your early game creatures to stall and dig for the combo, as they lack the graveyard hate to completely shut you down.

Use discard spells to force counterspells out of hand, then cast your reanimation spells when they're empty. Sequence your plays to make the opponent guess, and use cheap threats to pressure them into using counters on non-critical spells. If they counter your reanimation, pivot to digging for backup threats or graveyard hate.

Apply early pressure with discard and cheap threats. Don't let them stabilize; use graveyard hate if available, or race them with reanimated threats. Prioritize disrupting their mana development with hand disruption, and be ready to answer their large early threats with removal or counterspells.

Stabilize with early discard and removal. Don't overcommit to reanimation until you have a board presence or can answer their threats. Use your graveyard as a resource to dig for answers, and close the game quickly once stabilized. If they go wide, prioritize board wipes or targeted removal to protect your life total.

Out-value them with card advantage and graveyard recursion. Force them to use resources on your threats, then reanimate bigger threats when they're tapped out. Use discard to disrupt their combo or lock pieces, and be patient if they have board wipes; wait for them to use them on your early threats before committing your finishers.

Win the graveyard war. Prioritize graveyard hate and targeted removal. Force the opponent to break their own resources with discard, and protect your graveyard with counterspells or protection spells. Win by out-resourcing their graveyard, answering their reanimation threats, or forcing them to mulligan into a dead hand.

common_threats: Watch for graveyard hate (exile effects, discard spells targeting your graveyard), board wipes, and counterspells.
Note when the opponent starts playing around your reanimation (e.g., holding up removal, playing exile effects).
Track their mana development to predict when they can answer your threats, and watch for landfall triggers or elemental synergies that could swing the board quickly.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: A novel Reanimator brew sits in a comfortable tier two under Cauldrons and Mountains , and has been brought back thanks to the excellent teamwork of two Universes Beyond cards: Superior Spider-Man , and Final Fantasy ‘s Ardyn, The Usurper .

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: You cast Superior Spider-Man a copy of Bringer of the Last Gift . Bringer then returns everything, including Ardyn.
At the beginning of combat, Ardyn brings back one of your utility creatures as a 5/5, and lets you swing with Bringer of the Last Gift and any other demons you might have
he turns your Bringer pile into an OTK

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: You cast Superior Spider-Man a copy of Bringer of the Last Gift . Bringer then returns everything, including Ardyn.
At the beginning of combat, Ardyn brings back one of your utility creatures as a 5/5, and lets you swing with Bringer of the Last Gift and any other demons you might have

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Superior Spider-Man is great for reanimating your creatures even when there's a very high likelihood that your opponent is going to be playing one of the best graveyard hate pieces in Standard: Agatha's Soul Cauldron .
